title: EEE-Bench 开放题
rewritten_question_text: Consider the periodic square wave in the figure shown. The ratio of the power in the 7th harmonic to the power in the 5th harmonic for this waveform is closest in value to ______.

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: Write the expression for the function shown in the figure in terms of unit step functions.
expected_answer: \( u(t) - u(t - T) + \frac{(t - T)}{T} u(t - T) - \frac{(t - 2T)}{T} u(t - 2T) \)

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: Two parallel-connected, three-phase, 50 Hz, 11 kV, star-connected synchronous machines A and B are operating as synchronous condensers. They together supply 50 MVAR to an 11 kV grid. The current supplied by both machines is equal. The synchronous reactances of machine A and machine B are 1\(\Omega\) and 3\(\Omega\), respectively. Assuming the magnetic circuit to be linear, the ratio of excitation current of machine A to that of machine B is ______.

title: EEE-Bench 开放题
rewritten_question_text: In the operational amplifier circuit shown in the figure, it is known that \(U_{OM}=\pm 12V\), \(R_1=10k\Omega\), \(R_2=R_3=R_F=20k\Omega\), \(U_1=3V\), and \(U_2=2V\). When switch \(S\) is closed to position 1, what is \(U_0\)?

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: Given the active electrical network shown in the figure, use the complex impedance method to find the transfer function \( \frac{U_0(s)}{U_i(s)} \).

title: Dataset Open Question
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, an ideal autotransformer is connected to a sinusoidal alternating current at the input. The output voltage can be adjusted by changing the position of the tap, which alters the number of turns at the output. A pilot light \( L \) is connected in parallel at the output, and a sliding rheostat \( R \) is connected in the output circuit. The position of the slider can change the resistance value of \( R \). Both meters are ideal AC meters. When switch \( S \) is closed, how does sliding the tap downward affect the input power?
expected_answer: When switch \( S \) is closed, sliding the tap downward decreases the input power

title: Magnetic Field Inside the Hollow Region
rewritten_question_text: An infinitely long hollow cylindrical conductor has inner radius \( a \) and outer radius \( b \). Its current is uniformly distributed over the conducting cross-section. What is the magnitude of the magnetic field \( B \) at distances \( r<a \) from the center axis?

title: Magnetic Field Within the Conducting Shell
rewritten_question_text: An infinitely long hollow cylindrical conductor has inner radius \( a \) and outer radius \( b \). Its current is uniformly distributed over the conducting cross-section. As the distance \( r \) increases through the conducting region \( a<r<b \), how does the magnitude of the magnetic field \( B \) change qualitatively?
expected_answer: It increases with \( r \), rising from zero at \( r=a \) to its maximum at \( r=b \).

title: Magnetic Field Outside the Conductor
rewritten_question_text: An infinitely long hollow cylindrical conductor has inner radius \( a \) and outer radius \( b \). Its current is uniformly distributed over the conducting cross-section. For points outside the conductor, \( r>b \), how does the magnitude of the magnetic field \( B \) depend on \( r \)?
expected_answer: It decreases inversely with distance, i.e. \( B \propto 1/r \).

title: EEE-Bench 开放题
rewritten_question_text: A band-limited low-pass signal \(x(t)\) of bandwidth 5 kHz is sampled at a sampling rate \(f_s\). The signal \(x(t)\) is reconstructed using the reconstruction filter \(H(f)\), whose magnitude response is shown in the image. The minimum sampling rate \(f_s\) (in kHz) for perfect reconstruction of \(x(t)\) is ______.
